frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: LIP Tin neurons accumulate motion evidence toward a contralateral choice

frameworks.name: population coding of decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: across-neuron variability around mean firing carries choice-accuracy information

frameworks.name: logistic decoder
frameworks.one_line: linear readout predicts choice accuracy from Tin population activity

frameworks.name: signal-detection / confidence as belief in being correct
frameworks.one_line: confidence defined as pre-feedback belief that one's choice is correct

core_question: Do the LIP neurons that accumulate evidence for a choice also carry information about whether that choice is correct?
key_assumptions: Tin neurons represent evidence accumulation toward contralateral target selection
confidence is the pre-feedback belief that one's choice is correct
choice accuracy is a usable proxy for decision confidence
manipulations: random dot motion strength in a reaction-time discrimination task
contralateral versus other choices relative to recording site
key_findings: shortly before a contralateral report, the Tin population contains information about choice accuracy
on average, Tin firing before the report is independent of reaction time and motion strength
individual Tin neurons consistently depart from the population mean in a structured way
a simple logistic decoder predicts choice accuracy from Tin population responses
interpretations: across-neuron variability around a stereotyped mean carries the confidence signal in LIP
the same Tin neurons that accumulate evidence also encode the accuracy of the resulting choice
